categories.name: Broken Access Control
categories.description: Failures in enforcing access control policies, allowing users to act outside their intended permissions. Includes IDOR, privilege escalation, CORS misconfiguration, and SSRF.

categories.name: Security Misconfiguration
categories.description: Missing or incorrect security hardening across the application stack, including default credentials, unnecessary features enabled, overly permissive cloud configurations, and missing security headers.

categories.name: Software Supply Chain Failures
categories.description: Vulnerabilities introduced through third-party components, build pipelines, CI/CD integrity, and dependency management failures. Covers typosquatting, compromised packages, and insecure build processes.
categories.changed_from_2021: NEW - evolved from A08:2021 Software and Data Integrity Failures with expanded supply chain focus

categories.name: Cryptographic Failures
categories.description: Failures related to cryptography that expose sensitive data, including use of weak algorithms, improper key management, insufficient entropy, and transmission of data in cleartext.

categories.name: Injection
categories.description: User-supplied data sent to an interpreter as part of a command or query without proper validation or escaping. Includes SQL injection, XSS, command injection, LDAP injection, and template injection.

categories.name: Insecure Design
categories.description: Flaws in the design and architecture of the application rather than implementation bugs. Includes missing threat modeling, insecure business logic, and lack of defense-in-depth patterns.

categories.name: Authentication Failures
categories.description: Weaknesses in authentication mechanisms that allow attackers to compromise passwords, keys, or session tokens, or exploit implementation flaws to assume other users' identities.
categories.changed_from_2021: Remains A07; renamed from Identification and Authentication Failures

categories.name: Data Integrity Failures
categories.description: Failures to verify the integrity of data and code, including insecure deserialization, insufficient verification of data authenticity, and trusting data without validation.
categories.changed_from_2021: Narrowed scope from A08:2021; supply chain aspects moved to A03:2025

categories.name: Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
categories.description: Insufficient logging, monitoring, and alerting that prevents detection of active breaches, delayed incident response, and inability to establish forensic evidence trails.

categories.name: Mishandling of Exceptional Conditions
categories.description: Improper handling of unexpected conditions, errors, and edge cases that can lead to undefined behavior, information disclosure, denial of service, or security bypass.
categories.changed_from_2021: NEW - replaces A10:2021 SSRF (moved to A01)
